commits:
[{'sha': '0d5ecfae6747bcf2b13ffe1a440eaf860b7930b6', 'message': 'Fix mounting sub-applications under APIRouter (#10180)

Sub-apps mounted on an APIRouter were silently dropped when the router
was included into a FastAPI app via include_router(), causing 404s for
any path under the mount (e.g. GET /api/subapi/sub).

Two changes:

1. APIRouter.mount() override — prepends self.prefix to the path before
   delegating to Router.mount(), matching the behaviour of add_api_route
   which already does `self.prefix + path`. This ensures the stored
   route.path is consistent whether the mount is registered on the router
   or on the app directly.

2. include_router() Mount branch — a new `elif isinstance(route, routing.Mount)`
   case forwards mounted sub-applications with `prefix + route.path`,
   exactly as is done for every other route type (Route, APIRoute,
   WebSocketRoute).

Both prefixes compose correctly: router-level prefix and include_router
prefix stack in the same way as for ordinary endpoints.

Fixes #10180'}]

    "description": "在 `APIRouter.mount` 方法中，当 `self.prefix` 为空字符串时，`self.prefix + path` 可能导致路径以双斜杠 `//` 开头。例如，如果 `APIRouter(prefix=\"\")` 调用 `mount(\"/subapi\", ...)`，生成的路径将是 `\"/subapi\"`，这是正确的。但如果 `APIRouter(prefix=\"/api\")` 调用 `mount(\"\", ...)`，生成的路径将是 `\"/api\"`，这也是正确的。然而，如果 `APIRouter(prefix=\"/api\")` 调用 `mount(\"/subapi\", ...)`，生成的路径将是 `\"/api/subapi\"`，这符合预期。问题在于，如果 `APIRouter(prefix=\"/api/\")`（注意末尾斜杠）调用 `mount(\"/subapi\", ...)`，生成的路径将是 `\"/api//subapi\"`，这可能导致意外的路由行为。虽然 FastAPI/Starlette 的路由器可能能够规范化路径，但显式处理路径连接以避免双斜杠是更健壮的做法。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/routing.py` 的 `APIRouter.mount` 方法中：`super().mount(self.prefix + path, app=app, name=name)`。这里直接进行字符串拼接，没有处理 `self.prefix` 和 `path` 之间的斜杠连接。",
    "advice": "建议在拼接路径时使用 `urljoin` 或类似逻辑来确保路径连接正确，避免双斜杠。例如，可以定义一个辅助函数来规范化路径连接：`def _join_paths(prefix: str, path: str) -> str: return (\"/\" + prefix.strip(\"/\") + \"/\" + path.strip(\"/\")).replace(\"//\", \"/\")` 或使用 `urljoin`。但考虑到项目背景（FastAPI 是一个高性能、易于使用的框架），应保持简单且与现有行为一致。检查 `add_api_route` 方法中是否已有类似处理。如果没有，可以添加路径规范化逻辑以确保健壮性。",

    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/routing.py` 的 `include_router` 方法中：`elif isinstance(route, routing.Mount):`。这里只检查了 `routing.Mount` 类型。",
    "advice": "建议检查 FastAPI 中是否存在 `APIMount` 类。如果存在，应更新条件以同时处理 `routing.Mount` 和 `routing.APIMount`（例如，使用 `isinstance(route, (routing.Mount, routing.APIMount))`）。如果不存在，当前实现是合理的。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

[{'sha': 'f6f4c7d0dae134e5d1cf147ba64f9222d7a789ae', 'message': "fix: include method in operation_id to prevent duplicates with multi-method routes

When a route is defined with multiple HTTP methods (e.g., methods=['POST', 'DELETE']),
the operation_id generation was only using the first method name, causing all
methods to get the same operation_id suffix. This resulted in duplicate operation IDs
in the OpenAPI schema and incorrect warnings.

Changes:
- Modified generate_unique_id() to accept an optional 'method' parameter
- When a specific method is provided, it includes that method in the operation_id
- Updated get_openapi_operation_metadata() in openapi/utils.py to pass the method
- Each HTTP method now gets its own unique operation_id

Fixes #13175"}, {'sha': '97acb87acfb8e183e74874e8aec4f21f109bd776', 'message': 'test: add test for duplicate operation ID fix with multi-method routes'}, {'sha': 'a93a92f187a8c985e71870f06624c4d2c01e12c5', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

[{'sha': '158d52945a256d66369a98a0d667121905290bf5', 'message': 'Fix form model losing model_fields_set for unsubmitted fields

When a Pydantic BaseModel is used as a Form parameter, fields absent from
the form submission were being pre-filled with their default values before
the model was constructed. This caused Pydantic to treat those defaults as
explicitly-set values, so model_fields_set incorrectly included fields the
client never sent.

The fix skips absent fields in _extract_form_body entirely, letting Pydantic
apply defaults through its own mechanism (which correctly leaves them out of
model_fields_set), matching the behaviour of JSON body endpoints.

commits:
[{'sha': '158d52945a256d66369a98a0d667121905290bf5', 'message': 'Fix form model losing model_fields_set for unsubmitted fields

When a Pydantic BaseModel is used as a Form parameter, fields absent from
the form submission were being pre-filled with their default values before
the model was constructed. This caused Pydantic to treat those defaults as
explicitly-set values, so model_fields_set incorrectly included fields the
client never sent.

The fix skips absent fields in _extract_form_body entirely, letting Pydantic
apply defaults through its own mechanism (which correctly leaves them out of
model_fields_set), matching the behaviour of JSON body endpoints.

Fixes #13399'}]

There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ', '@garagon, thanks for your interest! Please, review existing PRs linked to the issue (including closed PRs, to understand the reasons they were closed) before opening your PRs. And provide an explanation why you think your approach is better. See https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/13944', '@YuriiMotov thanks for the pointer. I reviewed all related PRs: - #13944 (yours, open): supports StaticFiles only, raises `FastAPIError` for other ASGI apps - #14032 (sujin502, closed): generic Mount support, closed for inactivity - #14924, #14926 (kimchikingdom, closed): generic Mount, you redirected to #13944 - #14793 (jonathan-fulton, open): generic Mount support - #14984 (fcarvajalbrown, closed), #15014 (Anandesh-Sharma, closed): same approach as ours I understand your position: sub-apps should be mounted to the root app, and only StaticFiles makes sense on a sub-router. Looking at the issue comments, there are real use cases beyond StaticFiles that users are requesting: - Mounting v1/v2 API versions under a shared prefix router - Serving Dash dashboards alongside API routes on the same router - Adding authentication dependencies to third-party ASGI apps via router - Modular app composition where teams own routers with their own sub-apps The current behavior silently drops Mount routes with no error and no warning. Your PR (#13944) solves this for StaticFiles and raises a clear error for everything else, which is better than silent failure. Our approach propagates all Mount routes with the correct prefix. The technical difference is one line: `self.mount(prefix + router.prefix + route.path, app=route.app, name=route.name)`. The reason `router.prefix` is needed is that `APIRoute.path` has the prefix baked in at creation time, but `Mount.path` does not. Both approaches are valid. If Sebastian decides StaticFiles-only is the right scope, #13944 is the right PR. If full Mount support is desired, the approach in this PR (and #14793) handles it. Happy to close this if #13944 is the direction the project wants to take.']

And provide an explanation why you think your approach is better.', '> @abishekgiri, thanks for your interest! > > Please, review existing PRs linked to the issue (including closed PRs, to understand the reasons they were closed) before opening your PRs. And provide an explanation why you think your approach is better. I reviewed the PRs linked to #13056: #14952, #14557, #14703, #14872, #15096, #15131, and #15142. My understanding is: - #14952 adds only a regression test. - #14703 moves resolution later via `get_type_hints(..., include_extras=True)` in `get_dependant()`, but the exact reproducer still failed there. - #14872 and #15142 try to resolve `ForwardRef` later in the pipeline, but in this bug the outer `Annotated[...]` structure has already been lost too early, so FastAPI never extracts `Depends(...)` correctly. - #15131 addresses the later OpenAPI failure in the Pydantic compat layer, but it does not fix the earlier dependency-extraction problem and changes a much broader area. - #14557 is closer, but it relies on manual parsing of `Annotated[...]` strings plus substring `eval`, which felt heavier and more fragile than necessary. - #15096 is the closest implementation-wise. The main difference is that it replaces unresolved inner names with `Any`. I chose this approach because it fixes the problem at the earliest shared point: `get_typed_annotation()`. Instead of keeping the whole annotation unresolved, or converting missing inner names to `Any`, this PR evaluates with a lenient namespace that preserves unresolved inner names as nested `ForwardRef(...)` objects. That means FastAPI still sees the outer `Annotated[...]` structure, can still extract `Depends(...)`, and does not lose the original type information unnecessarily. I think that is better because: - it is a smaller and more local change - it fixes both runtime dependency extraction and OpenAPI generation from the same root cause - it avoids special-case parsing of `Annotated[...]` - it preserves unresolved types as `ForwardRef(...)` instead of degrading them to `Any` I also added a focused regression test for the exact issue shape from #13056, covering both runtime behavior and OpenAPI generation. ', "I looked into this and ended up with the same `dict.__missing__` → `ForwardRef` idea. One thing worth considering: using the lenient dict as a fallback instead of replacing the existing eval path. Right now you're routing *all* annotations through `_LenientTypeResolutionDict`, but `evaluate_forwardref` already works fine for the common case. The lenient path only needs to kick in when normal resolution returns an unresolved `ForwardRef`: ```python def get_typed_annotation(annotation: Any, globalns: dict[str, Any]) -> Any: if isinstance(annotation, str): annotation = ForwardRef(annotation) annotation = evaluate_forwardref(annotation, globalns, globalns) if isinstance(annotation, ForwardRef): try: annotation = eval( annotation.__forward_arg__, _LenientTypeResolutionDict(globalns), ) except Exception: pass if annotation is type(None): return None return annotation ``` Also — `self[key] = value` in `__missing__` caches into the dict during eval. If `evaluate_forwardref` does multiple passes internally, a cached `ForwardRef` could shadow a name that would resolve on a later pass. Probably fine since the dict is single-use, but worth checking."]

[{'sha': 'c5bd35c728c32fc7aa28fd650e7673678dd9d3a1', 'message': 'fix: treat empty bearer token as missing credentials in OAuth2 helpers

Authorization: Bearer  (empty token after "Bearer") was returned as ""
instead of triggering the auto_error path. Both OAuth2PasswordBearer and
OAuth2AuthorizationCodeBearer only checked that the scheme was "bearer"
but not that the credential string was non-empty.

RFC 6750 Section 2.1 defines b64token = 1*(...) — at least one character
required. An empty credential is syntactically malformed and should be
treated the same as missing credentials: 401 with auto_error=True, None
with auto_error=False.

Fix: add `not param` to the guard condition in both __call__ methods.

Closes: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/discussions/15192'}, {'sha': 'ca96fdf2922f0d08bfe468c78024ccedfa6fb937', 'message': "Merge branch 'master' into fix/oauth2-empty-bearer"}]

[{'sha': '7c4b6412438d1059f623a637e8ebc7efc6b29694', 'message': 'Fix duplicate operationId for multi-method routes

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods via
add_api_route(..., methods=["POST", "DELETE"]), generate_unique_id()
only uses the first method from the set, causing all methods to share
the same unique_id and producing duplicate operationIds in the OpenAPI
schema along with a UserWarning.

Fix by detecting multi-method routes in get_openapi_operation_metadata
and building a per-method operationId using the current method parameter
(already available in the function), following the same pattern as
generate_unique_id.

Fixes #13175'}, {'sha': 'b2e6f8dc1ec9bc1af69e4b8264829f79cd9eb98a', 'message': 'Add test for unique operationId generation in multi-method routes'}, {'sha': '937fcdffbeef192f8236442ed098dc768ab0696d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

Please, describe why this approach is better Yes, I reviewed the linked PRs before submitting. **Why this approach differs from rejected PRs:** - **#15070**: Only sorted methods for determinism, didn't make IDs unique per method — not a real fix. - **#15074 / #15140**: Appended method to `route.unique_id`, producing double suffixes like `do_something__put_put`. Our approach rebuilds the base from `route.name + route.path_format` using the same pattern as `generate_unique_id`, so the result is clean: `do_something__put` and `do_something__delete`. - **#10694**: Modifies routing layer. Our fix is more minimal — it stays in `get_openapi_operation_metadata()` where `method` is already available, touching only the OpenAPI generation step. **Known limitation**: The body schema name (`Body_do_something__put`) is still generated at route compilation time and remains non-deterministic for multi-method routes. This would require a separate fix at the routing layer (as #10694 attempts). Our PR addresses only the operationId duplication. Happy to adjust the approach based on your feedback. "]

[{'sha': 'a1a7e1f7b65c59f3e1fea97eb927c9a6b2cdd0a9', 'message': 'fix(routing): thread stream_item_type through include_router()

When a typed SSE or JSONL streaming endpoint is registered via a router,
the OpenAPI schema loses the stream item type because include_router()
re-creates routes via add_api_route() without passing stream_item_type.

This fix threads stream_item_type through add_api_route() and include_router(),
following the strict_content_type pattern.

Fixes #15077'}]

[{'sha': '58afccb27518c739afe09f6c2b32a88c1aa20c47', 'message': "Fix: clarify 'Required, can be None' query parameter docs

The documentation incorrectly stated that clients could send None as
a query parameter value. In reality, omitting a required parameter
returns 422, and there is no standard HTTP mechanism to pass None
as a query string value.

Changes:
- Updated docs to clarify that None in the type annotation means
  the OpenAPI schema marks the parameter as nullable
- Added info box explaining the actual behavior
- Fixed xfail tests to verify correct 422 behavior instead of
  expecting impossible 200 responses

Closes #12419"}]

commits:
[{'sha': '11dc2808067acb71771729205f124570d7205835', 'message': "Fix duplicate operation IDs for routes with multiple methods

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods (e.g., methods=['POST', 'DELETE']),
the default generate_unique_id function only uses the first method, causing duplicate
operation IDs in the OpenAPI schema.

This fix ensures that when a route has multiple methods and no explicit operation_id,
each method gets a unique operation ID by appending the current method name.

Fixes #13175"}]

[{'sha': '11dc2808067acb71771729205f124570d7205835', 'message': "Fix duplicate operation IDs for routes with multiple methods

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods (e.g., methods=['POST', 'DELETE']),
the default generate_unique_id function only uses the first method, causing duplicate
operation IDs in the OpenAPI schema.

This fix ensures that when a route has multiple methods and no explicit operation_id,
each method gets a unique operation ID by appending the current method name.

Fixes #13175"}]

[{'sha': '8bfab526a2c015765fb708dd2c63d816a4461edb', 'message': 'fix: reject newline characters in SSE event and id fields

`format_sse_event()` interpolated the `event` and `id` fields into SSE
wire-format bytes without stripping or rejecting newline characters. A
`\n` or `\r\n` in either field injects an additional SSE field-line into
the stream — enabling event-type spoofing and fabricated data payloads
in browser EventSource clients.

The `data` and `comment` fields were already protected via `splitlines()`;
the omission for `event` and `id` was an inconsistency, not a design
decision. The identical bug class was fixed in Hono (GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr).

Changes:
- Add `_check_event_no_newline()` validator and apply it to
  `ServerSentEvent.event` via `AfterValidator`
- Extend `_check_id_no_null()` to also reject `\n` and `\r`
- Strip `\r`/`\n` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` and `id` as
  defense-in-depth (low-level function, bypasses model validators)
- Add 7 regression tests covering LF/CR rejection in both the model
  and the low-level formatting function

Severity: Medium (client-side injection; server unaffected)
CWE: CWE-116 — Improper Encoding for Output in a Different Plaintext Context
OWASP: A03:2021 — Injection
Prior art: Hono GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr (same class, same fix pattern)'}, {'sha': '06eccbf077b76309382371fe9440083bea350d54', 'message': "fix: add null-byte protection to SSE 'event' field for symmetry with 'id'

`_check_event_no_newline` lacked a `\0` check that `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`
has had since the original implementation. Similarly, `format_sse_event()` stripped
`\0` from `id` but not from `event`.

Changes:
- Extend `_check_event_no_newline` to also reject `\0` characters
- Strip `\0` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` (mirrors existing `id` behavior)
- Add `test_server_sent_event_null_event_rejected` regression test

`event` and `id` are now fully symmetric in both the model validator and the
low-level wire formatter."}, {'sha': '9e51a7d6956376104e9ca31a09322083671be9a2', 'message': "fix: raise ValueError in format_sse_event instead of stripping invalid chars

Replace silent .replace() sanitization in format_sse_event() with explicit
ValueError raises for \n, \r, and \0 in the event and id fields.

Previously, format_sse_event() would strip those characters, creating a
behavioral inconsistency with ServerSentEvent's AfterValidator which raises
on the same input. As YuriiMotov pointed out: data allows newlines by SSE
spec design (each line becomes a separate data: frame), but event and id are
single-token fields where a newline is always a protocol violation. Silent
stripping masks bugs at the call site; raising surfaces them where they belong.

Both code paths now fail identically for the same invalid input. Tests updated
from strip-behavior assertions to ValueError assertions, plus two new null-byte
tests covering format_sse_event() directly for event and id."}]

  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 中，使用 `importlib.import_module` 导入 `ujson` 和 `orjson` 模块时，如果模块不存在，会抛出 `ModuleNotFoundError` 异常。然而，在异常处理中，将模块变量赋值为 `None` 后，后续代码可能会尝试访问这些变量的属性（例如 `orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS`），导致 `AttributeError`。原代码使用 `ImportError` 捕获，而新代码使用 `ModuleNotFoundError`，虽然 `ModuleNotFoundError` 是 `ImportError` 的子类，但捕获范围更窄，可能无法捕获所有导入错误。此外，`typing.cast` 的使用在模块为 `None` 时会导致类型不匹配。",
    "evidence": "文件: fastapi/responses.py
@@ -11,16 +12,28 @@
 from starlette.responses import StreamingResponse as StreamingResponse  # noqa
 from typing_extensions import deprecated
 
+
+class _UjsonModule(Protocol):
+    def dumps(self, __obj: Any, *, ensure_ascii: bool = ...) -> str: ...
+
+
+class _OrjsonModule(Protocol):
+    OPT_NON_STR_KEYS: int
+    OPT_SERIALIZE_NUMPY: int
+
+    def dumps(self, __obj: Any, *, option: int = ...) -> bytes: ...
+
+
 try:
-    import ujson
-except ImportError:  # pragma: nocover
-    ujson = None  # type: ignore
+    ujson = cast(_UjsonModule, importlib.import_module("ujson"))
+except ModuleNotFoundError:  # pragma: nocover
+    ujson = None  # type: ignore  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
 
 
 try:
-    import orjson
-except ImportError:  # pragma: nocover
-    orjson = None  # type: ignore
+    orjson = cast(_OrjsonModule, importlib.import_module("orjson"))
+except ModuleNotFoundError:  # pragma: nocover
+    orjson = None  # type: ignore  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]",
    "advice": "建议恢复使用 `ImportError` 来捕获更广泛的导入异常，或者确保在模块为 `None` 时，后续代码不会尝试访问其属性。同时，考虑使用 `Optional` 类型注解来明确变量可能为 `None`，并在访问前进行空值检查。",
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },

    "evidence": "文件: tests/test_default_response_class.py
@@ -1,15 +1,18 @@
 from typing import Any
 
-import orjson
 from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI
 from fastapi.responses import HTMLResponse, JSONResponse, PlainTextResponse
 from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
 
+from tests.utils import needs_orjson
+
 
 class ORJSONResponse(JSONResponse):
     media_type = "application/x-orjson"
 
     def render(self, content: Any) -> bytes:
+        import orjson
+
         return orjson.dumps(content)",
    "advice": "建议将 `import orjson` 移回文件顶部，并使用 `pytest.importorskip` 或 `needs_orjson` 装饰器来跳过测试，而不是在函数内部导入。这样可以保持代码结构清晰，并避免不必要的运行时导入开销。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 中，异常处理块内的注释 `# ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]` 存在拼写错误，应为 `# type: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]`。这可能导致类型检查器无法正确识别该注释。",
    "evidence": "文件: fastapi/responses.py
@@ -11,16 +12,28 @@
 from starlette.responses import StreamingResponse as StreamingResponse  # noqa
 from typing_extensions import deprecated
 
+
+class _UjsonModule(Protocol):
+    def dumps(self, __obj: Any, *, ensure_ascii: bool = ...) -> str: ...
+
+
+class _OrjsonModule(Protocol):
+    OPT_NON_STR_KEYS: int
+    OPT_SERIALIZE_NUMPY: int
+
+    def dumps(self, __obj: Any, *, option: int = ...) -> bytes: ...
+
+
 try:
-    import ujson
-except ImportError:  # pragma: nocover
-    ujson = None  # type: ignore
+    ujson = cast(_UjsonModule, importlib.import_module("ujson"))
+except ModuleNotFoundError:  # pragma: nocover
+    ujson = None  # type: ignore  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
 
 
 try:
-    import orjson
-except ImportError:  # pragma: nocover
-    orjson = None  # type: ignore
+    orjson = cast(_OrjsonModule, importlib.import_module("orjson"))
+except ModuleNotFoundError:  # pragma: nocover
+    orjson = None  # type: ignore  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]",
    "advice": "将注释 `# ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]` 更正为 `# type: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]`，以确保类型检查器能够正确解析。",
    "confidence": 0.95,

  }

    "description": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 中，新增的 `ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]` 注释语法不正确。在 Python 的类型注释中，`# type: ignore` 可以附带特定的错误代码，但 `ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]` 不是标准的 mypy 或类型检查器语法。这可能导致类型检查器（如 mypy）无法正确忽略该行，从而引发类型错误警告。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 的变更中，第 27 行和第 33 行使用了 `# ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]`。",
    "advice": "应使用标准的 `# type: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]` 语法。或者，如果目的是忽略整个赋值语句的类型，可以使用 `# type: ignore`。请根据项目已有的类型忽略风格进行修正。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 中，新增的 `Protocol` 类定义 `_UjsonModule` 和 `_OrjsonModule` 缺少必要的 `__call__` 或方法签名细节，可能导致类型提示不完整或误导。虽然 `Protocol` 用于结构化子类型，但 `ujson.dumps` 和 `orjson.dumps` 的实际签名可能更复杂（例如，`orjson.dumps` 的 `option` 参数有默认值）。当前定义过于简化，可能无法准确捕获外部库的接口，影响类型检查的准确性。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 的变更中，第 13-22 行定义了 `_UjsonModule` 和 `_OrjsonModule` Protocol。",
    "advice": "建议参考 `ujson` 和 `orjson` 的实际公共 API 签名，完善 `Protocol` 定义。例如，`orjson.dumps` 的 `option` 参数可能默认为 `None`。可以查看这些库的源码或类型存根以获取准确签名。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 中，将 `ujson` 和 `orjson` 的导入从 `try...except ImportError` 改为 `try...except ModuleNotFoundError` 并使用 `importlib.import_module` 是合理的，因为 `ModuleNotFoundError` 是 `ImportError` 的子类，更精确。然而，新增的 `cast` 操作和 `Protocol` 定义引入了更复杂的类型逻辑，这与文件中其他简单导入风格（如直接 `import ujson`）不一致。虽然这提升了类型安全性，但可能增加了代码的认知负担。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/responses.py` 的变更中，第 24-33 行将导入逻辑改为使用 `importlib.import_module` 和 `cast`。",
    "advice": "考虑到 FastAPI 项目强调“易于使用和学习”，这种变更虽然技术正确，但应确保整个文件或相关模块的风格保持一致。如果这是为了更好的类型提示，可以接受，但需确认项目其他部分是否有类似模式。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `tests/test_default_response_class.py` 中，新增的 `needs_orjson` 装饰器被应用于多个测试函数，但 `ORJSONResponse` 类的 `render` 方法内部动态导入了 `orjson`（第 13 行：`import orjson`）。如果 `orjson` 未安装，`needs_orjson` 装饰器会跳过测试，这避免了 `ModuleNotFoundError`。然而，在 `test_app`、`test_router_a` 和 `test_router_a_a` 测试中，它们使用的是自定义的 `ORJSONResponse` 类，该类内部导入了 `orjson`。如果 `needs_orjson` 跳过测试，则不会执行到内部导入，这没问题。但 `test_app_override` 和 `test_router_a_override` 测试没有使用 `ORJSONResponse`，却未被标记为需要 `orjson`，这可能导致不一致。不过，这些测试不依赖 `orjson`，所以当前没有 bug。但变更中为部分测试添加 `@needs_orjson` 可能遗漏了其他相关测试，需要确保所有依赖 `orjson` 的测试都被正确标记。",
    "evidence": "在 `tests/test_default_response_class.py` 的变更中，第 121、136、151 行添加了 `@needs_orjson` 装饰器，但 `test_app_override` 和 `test_router_a_override` 未添加。",
    "advice": "审查所有测试，确保任何直接或间接依赖 `orjson` 或 `ujson` 的测试都使用相应的 `@needs_orjson` 或 `@needs_ujson` 装饰器。如果测试不依赖这些库，则无需添加。",
    "confidence": 0.6,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

When using from __future__ import annotations with Annotated[Potato, Depends(...)],
the annotation can reach analyze_param as a ForwardRef. Previously only str was
resolved; ForwardRef was returned as-is, causing Depends to be missed and the
param to be treated as a Query param. This led to PydanticUserError during
OpenAPI schema generation.

Extend get_typed_annotation to also resolve ForwardRef before returning.'}]

commits:
[{'sha': '2ea91122c9960de437498a5c7ac83cca4a064cfc', 'message': 'Emit UserWarning when Response.background silently discards injected BackgroundTasks

When an endpoint injects BackgroundTasks via dependency injection AND
returns a Response that already has its own `background` attribute set,
the injected tasks are silently dropped. This is a confusing footgun
that causes tasks to disappear without any indication.

This change adds a UserWarning in that scenario so the silent data loss
becomes visible. The warning message explains how to resolve the
conflict (either use the injected BackgroundTasks exclusively, or stop
injecting BackgroundTasks when using Response.background directly).

Ref: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/11215

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>'}, {'sha': 'cb0c5f89afdc062531bf68ab471c141d2ac57f0d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

    "evidence": "def evaluate_forwardref(\n    value: Any,\n    globalns: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    localns: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n) -> Any:\n    # eval_type_lenient has been deprecated since Pydantic v2.10.0b1 (PR #10530)\n    try_eval_type = getattr(_pydantic_typing_extra, \"try_eval_type\", None)\n    if try_eval_type is not None:\n        return try_eval_type(value, globalns, localns)[0]\n    return _pydantic_typing_extra.eval_type_lenient(  # ty: ignore[deprecated]\n        value, globalns, localns\n    )",

review_comments:
['Added "no cover" because this line is only used for older versions of Pydantic. Ideally, I'd love to add a test configuration to test Pydantic 2.7.0 instead on the CI, to ensure we never break older, supported versions, cf "open question" in PR description ☝️ ', "LGTM, but **note that this change is breaking**. --- Alternatively we can probably do something like (in `encoders.py`): ```py try: from pydantic.color import Color # ty: ignore[deprecated] except ImportError: # pragma: no cover class Color: pass try: from pydantic_extra_types.color import Color as PyExtraColor except ImportError: # pragma: no cover class PyExtraColor: pass ... ENCODERS_BY_TYPE: dict[type[Any], Callable[[Any], Any]] = { bytes: lambda o: o.decode(), Color: str, # ty: ignore[deprecated] PyExtraColor: str, ... ``` (I haven't checked it) This way we support both `Color` implementations ", 'LGTM Just a note: this way we lose type hints for `evaluate_forwardref`. Not sure if it's important. As an alternative, we can try something like: ``` try: from pydantic._internal._typing_extra import try_eval_type except: try_eval_type = None try: from pydantic._internal._typing_extra import eval_type_lenient except: eval_type_lenient = None def evaluate_forwardref( value: Any, globalns: GlobalsNamespace | None = None, localns: MappingNamespace | None = None, ) -> Any: if try_eval_type is not None: return try_eval_type(value, globalns, localns)[0] if eval_type_lenient is not None: return eval_type_lenient(value, globalns, localns) raise RuntimeError("Both try_eval_type and eval_type_lenient are None") # pragma: no cover # Should never happen ``` But not sure it's worth it', 'I investigated this. Currently tests don't run on lowest Pydantic version (2.7) because of `pydantic-ai` optional dependency. `uv` fails to understand that this dependency is optional and considers it when resolving dependencies. So, as `pydantic-ai` depends on `pydantic 2.12`, it installs `pydantic 2.12`. I didn't manage to find a way to solve this.
``` [project] name = "test_resolution" version = "0.1.0" description = "Add your description here" readme = "README.md" requires-python = ">=3.10" dependencies = [ "pydantic>=2.7.0", ] [dependency-groups] translations = [ "pydantic-ai>=1.68.0", # Requires Pydantic 2.12 ] ``` `uv sync --no-dev --resolution lowest-direct` This will install pydantic 2.12 (even though `pydantic-ai` is not installed). Comment `pydantic-ai` dependency and it will install `pydantic` 2.7 (you will need to delete `uv.lock`)
So, maybe for now we can just manually add such configuration in test matrix?', 'For `pydantic_extra_types.color`, this will fail on `master` with > ValueError: [TypeError("'Color' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')] ', "Thanks for catching this @YuriiMotov! You're totally right. I commited your suggestion and added a unit test that fails on `master`, so we now support both `Color` classes explicitely.", 'Separated this into its own PR: #15139', "I do think it's an improvement to have the types here explicitely. `GlobalsNamespace` and `MappingNamespace` are typing variants of `dict[str, Any]` and `Mapping[str, Any]` respectively, defined in [_namespace_utils.py](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/blob/main/pydantic/_internal/_namespace_utils.py). This file only exists since Pydantic 2.10 though. In Pydantic 2.9, `eval_type_lenient` was typed twice with `dict[str, Any]`. So I've opted here to use `dict[str, Any]` as well, to support all Pydantic versions and not requireing external imports and more if/else logic. What do you think?", "As an alternative, we can use `try...except`: ```py try: return _pydantic_typing_extra.try_eval_type(value, globalns, localns)[0] except AttrbuteError: return _pydantic_typing_extra.eval_type_lenient( # ty: ignore[deprecated] value, globalns, localns ) # pragma: no cover ``` Or move this to module level, and only use it inside `evaluate_forwardref` (so that `evaluate_forwardref` would make it typed). It might also be a bit faster (but it's only used at app stratup, so maybe not as important)", '> For pydantic_extra_types.color, this will fail on master with > > ValueError: [TypeError("'Color' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have dict attribute')] > I took me some time to understand this comment :sweat_smile: Just to clarify if somebody also gets confused: it fails on `master` because `pydantic_extra_types.color.Color` is not currently supported by `jsonable_encoder`. This PR adds such support', "Yea I think it's a style preference, I'm personally more fan of checking explicitely first rather than relying on a `try`-`except`, but both are valid options. I'll leave it up to Sebastián to decide if he's got a preference 🙂 ", 'Great work @svlandeg! Clean approach to handling both deprecations 👍 A couple of thoughts: 1. **`Color` scoping** — Since `Color` is only used in `encoders.py` for the `ENCODERS_BY_TYPE` dict, the try/except fallback could live directly there instead of routing through `_compat/__init__.py`. That keeps the compat layer focused on core Pydantic abstractions (model fields, type evaluation, schema generation) rather than becoming a catch-all for deprecated imports. Minor though. 2. **Optional dep risk** — `pydantic-extra-types` is an optional dependency (under `standard`/`all`), so the `pydantic.color.Color` fallback is genuinely needed. Worth a small TODO comment noting that if Pydantic removes `pydantic.color` entirely in a future major, this fallback breaks silently. 3. **Success boolean from `try_eval_type`** — The lambda correctly discards it (matching `eval_type_lenient` behavior), but worth noting as a potential follow-up: `get_typed_annotation()` in `dependencies/utils.py` could use that boolean for better error messages when ForwardRef resolution fails silently. 4. **Naming nit** — `define_forwardref()` reads like it defines a forward ref, something like `_get_forwardref_evaluator()` might be clearer about what it actually does (selects the right eval function). Very minor. None of these are blockers — the core logic is solid, the `try_eval_type` wrapper is correct, and CI is all green. Really like the idea of proactively addressing these before `ty` enforcement lands. The open question about adding a Pydantic 2.7.0 CI run is a good one too — the `lowest-direct` matrix entry might already cover it, worth verifying. LGTM! 🚀', 'LGTM! Added optional suggestion for handling `Color`', 'LGTM!', ' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing svlandeg:feat/depr (e0e2568) with master (aeb9f4b)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (cbd64b0) during the generation of this report, so aeb9f4b was used instead as the comparison base. There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ', 'This pull request has a merge conflict that needs to be resolved.', '@AhsanSheraz: thanks for the review! Did you write this, or did you produce this with an LLM?', '> @AhsanSheraz: thanks for the review! Did you write this, or did you produce this with an LLM? You are welcome. Base message written by me, just format and make it more nice with gpt', "FYI - I don't think there's a need to format your reviews with an LLM. Genuine, human reviews are more valuable. Not to say you can't use LLMs - I do too in reviewing, to double check whether I've missed anything. But ultimately, a human's proper, in-depth review is worth more (IMO).", "> FYI - I don't think there's a need to format your reviews with an LLM. Genuine, human reviews are more valuable. Not to say you can't use LLMs - I do too in reviewing, to double check whether I've missed anything. But ultimately, a human's proper, in-depth review is worth more (IMO).   {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/_compat/v2.py` 中，新增的 `evaluate_forwardref` 函数在 `try_eval_type` 不可用时，回退到 `eval_type_lenient` 函数。然而，`eval_type_lenient` 函数在 Pydantic v2.10.0b1 之后已被标记为 deprecated，并且其导入路径已从 `pydantic._internal._typing_extra` 移动到 `pydantic._internal._typing_extra` 内部。代码中使用了 `_pydantic_typing_extra.eval_type_lenient`，但 `_pydantic_typing_extra` 是从 `pydantic._internal` 导入的 `_typing_extra` 模块。如果 `eval_type_lenient` 在 `_pydantic_typing_extra` 模块中不可用（例如，在 Pydantic 的某些版本中它可能已被移除或重命名），那么回退路径将引发 `AttributeError`，导致函数失败。这违反了项目对健壮性和向后兼容性的目标。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/_compat/v2.py` 中，新增的 `evaluate_forwardref` 函数定义如下：
```python
def evaluate_forwardref(
    value: Any,
    globalns: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    localns: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
) -> Any:
    # eval_type_lenient has been deprecated since Pydantic v2.10.0b1 (PR #10530)
    try_eval_type = getattr(_pydantic_typing_extra, "try_eval_type", None)
    if try_eval_type is not None:
        return try_eval_type(value, globalns, localns)[0]
    return _pydantic_typing_extra.eval_type_lenient(  # ty: ignore[deprecated]
        value, globalns, localns
    )
```
证据表明，当 `try_eval_type` 不存在时，代码直接调用 `_pydantic_typing_extra.eval_type_lenient`，但未检查 `eval_type_lenient` 是否存在。",
    "advice": "在回退到 `eval_type_lenient` 之前，应该使用 `getattr` 检查其是否存在，如果不存在，则提供一个更安全的回退或引发一个更清晰的错误。例如：
```python
    eval_type_lenient_func = getattr(_pydantic_typing_extra, "eval_type_lenient", None)
    if eval_type_lenient_func is not None:
        return eval_type_lenient_func(value, globalns, localns)
    # 如果两个函数都不存在，可能引发一个更合适的错误或尝试其他方法
    raise RuntimeError("Cannot evaluate forward ref: no suitable function found in Pydantic")
```
这确保了代码在 Pydantic 的不同版本中更加健壮。",
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/encoders.py` 中，新增的代码尝试导入 `pydantic_extra_types.color.Color` 作为 `PyExtraColor`。如果导入失败，则定义一个空的 `PyExtraColor` 类作为占位符。然而，这个占位符类被添加到 `ENCODERS_BY_TYPE` 字典中，其编码器为 `str`。如果 `pydantic_extra_types` 不可用，`PyExtraColor` 将是一个空类，永远不会被实例化，因此将其添加到编码器字典中是多余的，可能会引起混淆。虽然这不会导致运行时错误（因为不会有该类型的实例），但它降低了代码的可读性和清晰度，因为读者可能会疑惑为什么为一个永远不会使用的类添加编码器。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/encoders.py` 中，新增的代码：
```python
try:
    # Supporting the new Color format for newer versions of Pydantic
    from pydantic_extra_types.color import Color as PyExtraColor
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover

    class PyExtraColor:  # type: ignore[no-redef]  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
        pass

...
ENCODERS_BY_TYPE: dict[type[Any], Callable[[Any], Any]] = {
    ...
    PyExtraColor: str,
}
```",
    "advice": "建议仅在成功导入 `PyExtraColor` 时才将其添加到 `ENCODERS_BY_TYPE` 字典中。这样可以避免为一个永远不会使用的占位符类添加条目，使代码更清晰。例如：
```python
try:
    from pydantic_extra_types.color import Color as PyExtraColor
    EXTRA_COLOR_AVAILABLE = True
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
    PyExtraColor = None
    EXTRA_COLOR_AVAILABLE = False

...
ENCODERS_BY_TYPE: dict[type[Any], Callable[[Any], Any]] = {
    ...
}
if EXTRA_COLOR_AVAILABLE:
    ENCODERS_BY_TYPE[PyExtraColor] = str
```",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "在 `fastapi/encoders.py` 中，新增的代码通过 try/except 块处理 `pydantic.color.Color` 和 `pydantic_extra_types.color.Color` 的导入，以支持 Pydantic 中 Color 类的不同版本和位置。然而，`pydantic.color.Color` 已被标记为 deprecated，并且项目背景强调“Standards-based”和“Robust”。虽然添加对 `pydantic_extra_types.color.Color` 的支持是合理的，但代码中同时保留了旧的 deprecated `Color` 的编码器。这符合项目向后兼容的目标，但需要注意，如果用户升级 Pydantic 到不再提供 `pydantic.color.Color` 的版本，旧的导入将失败。代码通过 try/except 处理了这种情况，但定义了一个空的 `Color` 类作为占位符。这个设计是合理的，但可能会在未来的 Pydantic 版本中导致类型检查问题，因为空的 `Color` 类可能不匹配实际 `Color` 类的接口。不过，由于编码器只是调用 `str()`，这可能是可接受的。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/encoders.py` 中，新增的代码：
```python
try:
    # pydantic.color.Color is deprecated since v2.0b3, but supporting for bwd-compat
    from pydantic.color import Color  # ty: ignore[deprecated]
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover

    class Color:  # type: ignore[no-redef]  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
        pass

try:
    # Supporting the new Color format for newer versions of Pydantic
    from pydantic_extra_types.color import Color as PyExtraColor
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover

    class PyExtraColor:  # type: ignore[no-redef]  # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment]
        pass

ENCODERS_BY_TYPE: dict[type[Any], Callable[[Any], Any]] = {
    ...
    Color: str,
    PyExtraColor: str,
}
```",
    "advice": "当前设计是合理的，因为它保持了向后兼容性并支持新的 Color 类。建议确保在文档或注释中说明对 Pydantic Color 类的支持策略，以便用户了解。此外，考虑在未来的版本中，当 Pydantic 的 deprecated `Color` 被完全移除时，移除对应的 try/except 块和占位符类。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

commits: [{'sha': '17f10eb0a8ca6f620964d6d871b18031a7129e8d', 'message': 'test: replace synthetic collector test with lifecycle integration and remove unreachable collector branch\n\nMade-with: Cursor'}, {'sha': '6215c768f2d029c9440dbfe12bc716468a143d5d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}, {'sha': 'c76bf51d49e04ea8c4bd9835d0d036fd57dfb9fb', 'message': "Merge branch 'master' into feature/lifespan-dependency-scope"}]
review_comments: ['`# pragma: no cover` is not needed here', 'Could you please explain why this is marked with `no cover`?', 'Could you please explain why `# pragma: no cover` is needed here?', 'Could you please explain why `# pragma: no cover` is needed here?', 'Could you please explain why `# pragma: no cover` is needed here?', 'You’re right, # pragma: no cover isn’t needed here. This code runs on every import in normal usage and is straightforward to exercise through existing tests.', 'Good point — these branches are part of the normal dependency‑flattening path and can be covered by tests, so excluding them isn’t justified. I’ll remove the # pragma: no cover markers and ensure we have tests that go through this code.', 'This was originally marked as no cover because the coverage gap came from wiring the new "lifespan" scope into the existing cache and I wanted to keep the change small while getting the feature working.', 'These helpers were marked no cover as defensive fallbacks for cases where the ASGI lifespan isn’t wired, so I wasn’t originally expecting them to run in normal tests. ', 'The intent here was to normalize all supported lifespan styles (async generator, sync generator, or already a context manager) into a single async context manager before wrapping it in the dependency cache, and I used no cover while getting coverage to 100%.', 'Before changes from this PR the coverage was 100%. If after changes these lines are not executed - we need to understand why, not just mark them with `# pragma: no cover`', "Addressed in the latest commits: the inner assignment no longer has a pragma. The only remaining pragma is on the whole `if sys.version_info >= (3, 14):` block so that combined coverage stays 100% (that branch only runs in the 3.14 job). I can remove it if you're comfortable with that path being covered only in the 3.14 matrix job.", 'Addressed: removed all pragmas from this block and added `test_collect_lifespan_dependants_route_level_scope` so this path is covered. No more no cover here.', "Addressed by adding `test_lifespan_dependency_shared_cache_hit` (two lifespan deps that share one dependency). The cache-hit line still has a single pragma because in our runs that path wasn't consistently hit (resolution order). Happy to remove it if the combined CI matrix covers it.", "Addressed: `noop_receive` is now covered by `test_lifespan_dependency_synthetic_request_receive_send`. `noop_send` still has one pragma — it's a defensive stub that's never called during lifespan dependency resolution. Happy to remove it if you prefer.", "Addressed: removed the pragmas and added two tests that use `APIRouter(lifespan=...)` with a raw async generator and a raw sync generator: `test_router_apirouter_raw_async_gen_lifespan` and `test_router_apirouter_raw_sync_gen_lifespan`. Those branches are now executed and covered, so we're not masking missing coverage with pragma.", 'We should try to avoid such kind of tests (where we call internal private functions just to improve coverage) and write test that are closer to real-life. You will not call `_collect_lifespan_dependants` in your app, right? So, what is the use case that would cause this code to be executed under the hood?', 'thanks for calling this out. i replaced that synthetic test with an integration-style test and removed the internal mutation path. Concretely: - Removed the test that called `_collect_lifespan_dependants()` directly and did `route.dependant.scope = "lifespan"`. - Added `test_lifespan_dependency_decorator_level_dependencies_runs_at_startup`, which uses public API only: `@app.get(..., dependencies=[Depends(..., scope="lifespan")])` + `TestClient(app)` lifecycle. I also re-checked the runtime flow and adjusted implementation accordingly: - `FastAPI.__init__` wraps lifespan with `_wrap_lifespan_with_dependency_cache(...)`. - Startup calls `_run_lifespan_dependencies(...)`. - That calls `_collect_lifespan_dependants(...)`. - In normal APIRoute flow, top-level route dependant is created with `scope="function"`, so the realistic collection path is via `flat.dependencies` entries with `computed_scope == "lifespan"`. Because of that, I removed the unreachable `flat.computed_scope == "lifespan"` branch from `_collect_lifespan_dependants` rather than forcing a synthetic test to hit it.', '@essentiaMarco, thanks for your interest! I took a quick look and the amount of `# pragma: no cover` looks suspicious. Could you please address my review comments? Also, could you please compare your approach with the approach in https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/12529. Do you think your approach is better? Could you please explain why?
